_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: GA state legislature batch (state tier): nikki-merritt (SD-9 D), emanuel-jones (SD-10 D), will-wade (HD-9 R), victor-anderson (HD-10 R), rick-jasperse (HD-11 R). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no public position. CANDIDACY CURRENCY: emanuel-jones did NOT seek Senate re-election, ran for US House GA-13 and LOST the 2026-05-19 Dem primary -> status lost. will-wade is vacating HD-9 and is the 2026 GOP nominee for State Senate District 51 (won 2026-06-16 runoff). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: rick-jasperse (R) voted NO on SB233 school vouchers.

profile.background: GA State Senator, District 9 (Grayson/Gwinnett), Democrat, since Jan 2021; re-elected 2024. 23-year AT&T telecom worker, CWA union member; Vice Chair of the GA Legislative Black Caucus. Active 2026 re-election candidate (same seat, primary 2026-05-19).

nikki-merritt@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator SD-9 (D), since 2021; active 2026 re-election candidate, same seat. [2026-06-20 national refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive record: rated pro-choice (Choice Tracker) + endorsed by Georgia WIN List (pro-choice PAC); voted NAY on SB39 (2025 ban on gender-affirming care under the state health plan), calling it 'bullying' a 'vulnerable group of kids'; opposes school vouchers (pro-public-school); leads pro-voting-access work (Henry McNeal Turner Voting Rights Act, opposing SB202-style restrictions); sponsored gun-control bills (SB40 limits on who may carry, SB119) and is a gun-violence-prevention 'legislative ally.' No cross-party (conservative) deviation found on any dimension. Religion/church: no evidence found. Cells with no public position left null.

0.note: Rated pro-choice (Choice Tracker); campaign advocates 'protecting abortion rights' and decriminalizing reproductive healthcare. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Endorsed by Georgia WIN List (pro-choice women's PAC); opposes abortion restrictions, not an abolitionist.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB39 (2025) banning gender-affirming care under the state health plan; called the GOP majority 'bullies' picking on 'a vulnerable group of kids.' Affirms transgender identity.

4.note: Opposes 'don't-say-gay' bills and supports LGBTQ promotion / healthcare access for trans teens.

0.note: Opposes 'public dollars funneled towards private schools through voucher programs'; committed to public education over school choice.

3.note: Co-leads the Henry McNeal Turner Voting Rights Act to expand voting access; opposes SB202-style restrictions on mail/drop-box/early voting.

0.note: Sponsored SB40 ('limits on who may carry firearms'); opposes permitless/constitutional carry. Gun-violence-prevention legislative ally.

1.note: Sponsored SB119 restricting gun access for family-violence offenders; gun-control side.

set.office: State Senator, SD-10 (outgoing; lost 2026 GA-13 congressional primary)

profile.religion: Baptist — deacon, Shiloh Baptist Church, McDonough
profile.background: GA State Senator, District 10 (DeKalb/Henry), Democrat, since 2005; term ends Jan 2027. U.S. Army Corps of Engineers officer; auto-dealership businessman; UPenn EE / Columbia MBA.

emanuel-jones@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator SD-10 (D), since 2005. [2026-06-20 national refinement, state tier] CANDIDACY: did NOT seek Senate re-election; ran for U.S. House GA-13 and LOST the 2026-05-19 Democratic primary to Jasmine Clark -> marked lost (outgoing, off the 2026 ballot; Senate term expires Jan 2027). Uniformly progressive record anchored by the Freedom Index 2024 GA scorecard (0%): voted NO on HB1018 (2nd-Amendment Protection Act) and champions gun safe-storage; NO on HB1105 (anti-sanctuary Criminal Alien Track and Report Act); YES on the 'Georgia Green New Deal' and student-loan forgiveness; opposes school vouchers (public-school funding); frames election-integrity laws as 'voter suppression'; voted NO on SB63 (cash-bail expansion). No cross-party (conservative) deviation found. Baptist deacon (Shiloh Baptist, McDonough). Cells with no public position left null.

0.note: Longtime Democrat with a pro-choice platform; in the Democratic bloc opposing GA's heartbeat law. Does not affirm personhood from conception.
Advocates increased funding for public schools; no support for vouchers/ESAs (opposes the SB233 Promise Scholarship approach).

2.note: Freedom Index 2024 GA score 0%: YES on the 'Georgia Green New Deal' (HB206) and student-loan forgiveness (HB872); pro-spending, not balanced-budget.

1.note: Says he 'led the charge to protect voting rights and combat voter suppression' — opposes voter-ID/SB202-style integrity laws.
Voted NO on SB63 (2023 cash-bail expansion / bail-fund restriction); on the bail-leniency side.

2.note: Voted NO on HB1105 (2024 Criminal Alien Track and Report Act / anti-sanctuary immigration enforcement).

1.note: Voted NO on HB1018 (2nd-Amendment Protection Act, 2024); champions firearm safe-storage. Gun-control side.

set.office: State Representative, HD-9 (sitting; 2026 GOP nominee for State Senate District 51)

profile.religion: Baptist (named 2023 Legislator of the Year by the Georgia Baptist Mission Board)
profile.background: GA State Representative, House District 9 (Dawson/Lumpkin/White), Republican, since Jan 2021; banker (SVP, River City Bank); former Dawson Co. Board of Education member and Kemp House floor leader. Vacating HD-9 — 2026 GOP nominee for State Senate District 51 (won the 2026-06-16 runoff 61%; faces Democrat Gary St. Lawrence on 2026-11-03).

will-wade@GA.notes_set: GA State Representative HD-9 (R), since 2021. [2026-06-20 national refinement, state tier] CANDIDACY: vacating HD-9; 2026 GOP nominee for State Senate District 51 (open seat; won the 2026-06-16 GOP runoff 61.2%-38.8%; general 2026-11-03). Documented conservative record concentrated in education/family: PRIMARY SPONSOR of HB1084 (2022 'divisive concepts'/anti-CRT school law) and sponsor of HB147 (Safe Schools Act) and SB42 (anti-trafficking); carries a 2026 Georgia Life Alliance pro-life certification; endorsed by seven SD-51 county sheriffs (law-and-order). Stated record (own campaign site) on blocking biological males from girls' sports and minor gender-transition. Banker; GA Baptist Mission Board Legislator of the Year (Baptist). Several marquee roll calls (HB481, SB319, HB1105) could not be individually verified and are left null; campaign 'passed RFRA' claim is unverified (no GA RFRA enacted) and not scored. No progressive deviation found.

0.note: Carries a 2026 Georgia Life Alliance pro-life certification; campaign states he 'fought to protect the unborn.'

2.note: States he 'blocked biological males from competing in girls' sports' and worked to 'prevent gender transition procedures for minors' (SB1/SB140-aligned). Affirms biological sex.
PRIMARY SPONSOR of HB1084 (2022), the 'divisive concepts'/anti-CRT school law (signed Apr 2022).

0.note: Endorsed by seven SD-51 county sheriffs (Pickens, Dawson, Lumpkin, Union, Fannin, White); 'Law & Order' platform; co-sponsored SB42 anti-trafficking. Affirmative pro-law-enforcement backing.

profile.religion: Baptist — deacon, B.C. Grant Baptist Church
profile.background: GA State Representative, House District 10 (Habersham/Rabun), Republican, since Jan 2021; re-elected 2024. Georgia Tech mechanical-engineering grad; small-business owner (Anderson Carpet One); former Habersham Co. commission chair. Active 2026 primary candidate.

victor-anderson@GA.notes_set: GA State Representative HD-10 (R), since 2021; active 2026 candidate. [2026-06-20 national refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative (Turning Point Action lifetime 100). HARD votes: YES on SB1 (2025 girls' sports) and SB185 (2025 minor medical-transition limits); YES on SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers, both years); YES on HB268 (Safe Classrooms Act); YES on SB36 (2025 Religious Freedom Restoration Act); YES on HB1001 (income-tax cut); YES on HB963 (foreign-national campaign-funding ban). iVoterGuide questionnaire: affirms man-woman marriage, opposes SOGI protected classes, pro-life (except life-of-mother -> restrictionist NOT abolitionist), favors border wall + defunding sanctuary jurisdictions, opposes voting without photo ID, supports armed teachers / no new gun restrictions. Deacon (B.C. Grant Baptist). No cross-party deviation; lone FALSE is the within-camp abolition distinction. Public-justice bail/prosecutor votes not found -> null.

0.note: iVoterGuide: pro-life, supports the Born-Alive Act, opposes taxpayer abortion funding.

1.note: Restrictionist with a life-of-mother exception, NOT an abortion abolitionist.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly agrees' marriage is the union of one man and one woman.

1.note: Affirms exclusive man-woman marriage in law; opposes redefinition.

2.note: YES on SB1 (girls' sports) and SB185 (minor medical-transition limits); GOP party-line YES on SB140 (2023). Rejects transgender ideology.

4.note: Opposes SOGI protected classes / LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: YES on SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship voucher/ESA), 2023 and 2024 final passage.

2.note: YES on HB268 (Safe Classrooms Act); opposes SOGI curricula (TPAction Educational Freedom 100%).

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly supports' protecting Christian religious expression.

1.note: YES on SB36 (2025 Religious Freedom Restoration Act) — conscience protection.
iVoterGuide: 'strongly opposes voting without photo ID.'

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly favors' a border wall + complete entry/exit control.
iVoterGuide: believes no new gun restrictions are needed; supports armed teachers (constitutional-carry aligned).

profile.background: GA State Representative, House District 11 (Gordon/Murray/Pickens), Republican, since 2010. Ag educator/county agent; UGA food science. Lead House sponsor of the 2014 'guns everywhere' Safe Carry Protection Act (HB60). Active 2026 candidate.

rick-jasperse@GA.notes_set: GA State Representative HD-11 (R), since 2010; active 2026 candidate. [2026-06-20 national refinement, state tier] Conservative on social/cultural/2A dimensions but an establishment Republican on fiscal/spending (Freedom Index ~50% cumulative). HARD votes (Freedom Index): YES on SB319 constitutional carry and lead sponsor of HB60 (2014 'guns everywhere'); YES on the 2022 trans-sports bill and SB140 (minor gender-transition limits); YES on the Parents' Bill of Rights; YES on the 2024 citizen-only-voting amendment; co-sponsor of HB1105 (anti-sanctuary). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted NO on SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers) — an R breaking with party leadership against school choice (biblical/family cell 0 FALSE). Also NO on the 'Right to IVF' codification (conservative-aligned). Self-stated pro-life, traditional marriage, school prayer, anti-red-flag, fiscal restraint. Marquee roll calls not individually verifiable (HB481, SB202, SB63) left null.

0.note: Self-identifies pro-life; states he supported the 2019 LIFE Act (HB481) and opposes taxpayer-funded and partial-birth abortion.

2.note: Voted NO on the 'Right to IVF' codification bill (Freedom Index logs the NO as the conservative/pro-embryo-protection position).
YES on the 2022 'Protect Students First Act' (trans-sports restriction) and the 2023 bill limiting medical/surgical gender transition for minors (SB140).

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted NO on SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers/ESA) — a Republican against his party's flagship school-choice bill.

1.note: YES on the 2022 Parents' Bill of Rights (curriculum transparency / parental rights).

3.note: Supports 'voluntary prayer in all schools' and 'our right to prayer.'

2.note: 'Georgia has a spending problem not a revenue problem'; voted NO on the 2022 budget increase and the 2020 online-sales-tax expansion; YES on the 2026 income-tax cut.

1.note: YES on the 2024 citizen-only-voting constitutional amendment (citizenship verification).

2.note: Co-sponsor of HB1105 (2024 Criminal Alien Track and Report Act) — anti-sanctuary, mandates local ICE cooperation, penalizes non-compliant sheriffs.

0.note: Lead sponsor of HB60 (2014 'guns everywhere' Safe Carry Protection Act); YES on SB319 (2022 constitutional carry).

1.note: Opposes red-flag laws as a due-process violation.
